heading: Aquatic ecosystem restoration
label: 2330 Aquatic ecosystem restoration
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1996, and not as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 which comprises this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: will improve the quality of the environment and is in the public interest; or

children.label: ii
children.text: will improve the elements and features of an estuary (as defined in ); andsection 2902 of this title

children.heading: Dam removal
children.label: 2 Dam removal
children.text: A project under this section may include removal of a dam.

children.label: i
children.text: installing fish bypass structures on small water diversions;

children.label: iii
children.text: restoring or reconnecting floodplains and wetlands that are important for anadromous fish habitat or passage.

children.heading: Benefits
children.label: B Benefits
children.text: A project that includes measures under this paragraph shall be formulated to maximize benefits for the anadromous fish species benefitted by the project.

children.heading: Drought resilience
children.label: 4 Drought resilience
children.text: A project under this section may include measures that enhance drought resilience through the restoration of wetlands or the removal of invasive species.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Non-Federal interests shall provide 35 percent of the cost of construction of any project carried out under this section, including provision of all lands, easements, rights-of-way, and necessary relocations.

children.heading: Form
children.label: 2 Form
children.text: Before , the Federal share of the cost of a project under this section may be provided in the form of reimbursements of project costs.October 1, 2003

children.heading: Anadromous fish
children.label: 3 Anadromous fish
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), for projects carried out under subsection (a)(3), the non-Federal interest shall provide 15 percent of the cost of construction, including provision of all lands, easements, rights-of-way, and necessary relocations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Construction of a project under this section shall be initiated only after a non-Federal interest has entered into a binding agreement with the Secretary to pay the non-Federal share of the costs of construction required by this section and to pay 100 percent of any operation, maintenance, and replacement and rehabilitation costs with respect to the project in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Nonprofit entities
children.label: 2 Nonprofit entities
children.text: Notwithstanding , for any project carried out under this section, a non-Federal interest may include a nonprofit entity, with the consent of the affected local government.section 1962d–5b of title 42

children.heading: Cost limitation
children.label: d Cost limitation
children.text: Not more than $15,000,000 in Federal funds may be allotted under this section for a project at any single locality.

children.heading: Use of natural and nature-based features
children.label: e Use of natural and nature-based features
children.text: In carrying out a project to restore and protect an aquatic ecosystem or estuary under subsection (a), the Secretary shall consider, and may include, with the consent of the non-Federal interest, a natural feature or nature-based feature, as such terms are defined in , if the Secretary determines that inclusion of such features is consistent with the requirements of subsection (a).section 2289a of this title

children.heading: Funding
children.label: f Funding
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $75,000,000 for each fiscal year.

children.heading: Prioritization
children.label: g Prioritization
children.text: The Secretary shall give projects that include measures described in subsection (a)(3) equal priority for implementation as other projects under this section.
